commit.message:
feat(ui): SSR per-request isolation via AsyncLocalStorage (#1013)

* feat(ui): add SSRRenderContext DI infrastructure + adapter migration [#1009]

Phase 1 of SSR per-request isolation:
- Add SSRRenderContext type and registerSSRResolver/getSSRContext DI pattern
- Migrate getAdapter() to check SSR context before module-level singleton
- Export resolver API from @vertz/ui/internals
- Add design plan document

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat(ui): migrate reactive runtime state to SSR context-aware [#1009]

Phase 2 of SSR per-request isolation:
- tracking.ts: get/setSubscriber, get/setReadValueCallback, untrack
  check getSSRContext() before module-level singletons
- disposal.ts: pushScope/popScope/onCleanup/_tryOnCleanup use
  per-request cleanupStack via getCleanupStack()
- scheduler.ts: batch/scheduleNotify use per-request batchDepth
  and pendingEffects

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat(ui): migrate context scope, stores, and query cache to SSR context-aware [#1009]

- context.ts: getContextScope/setContextScope check SSRRenderContext
- entity-store-singleton.ts: getEntityStore/getQueryEnvelopeStore check SSRRenderContext
- query.ts: getDefaultCache()/getInflight() helpers replace direct singleton refs
- SSRRenderContext type extended with entityStore, envelopeStore, queryCache, inflight
- Test: entity store and query envelope store isolation per request

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat(ui): consolidate SSR detection — remove globalThis hooks [#1009]

Replace all globalThis.__VERTZ_* hooks with getSSRContext() calls:
- __VERTZ_IS_SSR__, __VERTZ_SSR_REGISTER_QUERY__, __VERTZ_GET_GLOBAL_SSR_TIMEOUT__
- __SSR_URL__, __VERTZ_CLEAR_QUERY_CACHE__, __VERTZ_CLEAR_ENTITY_STORE__
- Router SSR path returns lightweight read-only router from ctx.url
- Add test-ssr-helpers.ts for @vertz/ui tests (enableTestSSR/disableTestSSR)
- Export EntityStore/QueryEnvelopeStore from internals for SSR context creation
- Update render-to-html.ts to use createRequestContext() for full SSR context
- Migrate all test files to use SSR context helpers

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat(ui): remove render lock + per-render DOM shim [#1009]

- Remove withRenderLock() mutex — no longer needed with per-request isolation
- Install DOM shim once at process startup via ensureDomShim() instead of per-render
- Make injectCSS() skip DOM injection in SSR (track in Set only for getInjectedCSS())
- Remove installSSR()/removeSSR() from render-to-html.ts
- Collect CSS via getInjectedCSS() instead of reading shared document.head

* feat(ui-server): per-request fetch scoping via AsyncLocalStorage [#1009]

- Add fetch-scope.ts with installFetchProxy() + runWithScopedFetch()
- Replace globalThis.fetch mutation in bun-dev-server.ts with scoped fetch
- Replace globalThis.fetch mutation in cloudflare/handler.ts with scoped fetch
- Add ./fetch-scope export to @vertz/ui-server package
- Stress test: 50 concurrent scopes correctly isolated

* fix(ui-server): fix dual-module SSR resolver + router test for per-request isolation [#1009]

- Bridge SOURCE↔BUILT module gap: register ALS-backed SSR resolver
  on SOURCE @vertz/ui's _ssrResolver for tests that use relative imports
- Install DOM shim at module level for tests creating routers outside SSR
- Update router test: create router per-render (inside SSR context)
  instead of module-level singleton — module-level routers lost their
  globalThis sync hook when per-request isolation removed all globals

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(ui): restore window check in createRouter for non-browser environments [#1009]

createRouter() needs to handle three environments:
1. SSR context active → lightweight per-request router (getSSRContext())
2. No SSR context, no window → lightweight router with initialUrl (Bun tests)
3. Browser → full reactive router with popstate/navigation

Phase 3 removed the `typeof window === 'undefined'` check, causing
createRouter() to crash with "window is not defined" in Bun tests
that call it without an active SSR context.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(ui): SSR-aware getters in createRouter for per-request URL isolation [#1009]

Module-level routers (created at import time) hit the lightweight SSR path
because `typeof window === 'undefined'` at module load time (DOM shim not
installed yet). The lightweight router returned plain data properties for
`current` and `searchParams`, ignoring the per-request SSR context URL.

Changed both the SSR-path and browser-path routers to use `get value()`
getters that check `getSSRContext()` and return per-request route matches.
This ensures module-level router singletons correctly resolve the request
URL during SSR rendering.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(ui-server): remove installDomShim early-return guard + update SSR tests [#1009]

The early-return guard in installDomShim() prevented shim installation when
`document` already existed (e.g., from happy-dom in test runners). On main,
the guard checked for __SSR_URL__ to bypass. The SSR consolidation commit
replaced this with ssrStorage.getStore() which returns undefined when called
outside ssrStorage.run() — causing the guard to fire and skip installation.

Removed the guard entirely since installDomShim() is deprecated and SSR-only.
ensureDomShim() already handles idempotency. The shim should always install
when explicitly called.

Updated task-manager SSR tests to use ssrRenderToString (the new per-request
API) instead of the old installDomShim + __SSR_URL__ approach. Added tests
for /settings, /tasks/new, and /tasks/:id to validate per-request URL isolation.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(ui-server): handle stale domShimInstalled flag after removeDomShim [#1009]

ensureDomShim() cached a flag but removeDomShim() could delete document
without resetting it. In CI, test ordering caused dom-shim tests to
remove the shim before ssr-handler tests ran, making ensureDomShim()
a no-op with no document available.

Now checks both the flag and typeof document to detect stale state.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* test(ui,ui-server): improve SSR coverage for navigate and dom-shim [#1009]

- Add 11 SSR-aware getter tests for createRouter (navigate.ts)
- Add SSRComment, SSRNode, SSRElement, toVNode coverage tests (dom-shim)
- Cover removeAttribute, textContent, innerHTML, firstChild, nextSibling
- Cover toVNode primitive fallback and innerHTML raw HTML output
- Cover installDomShim with existing window (location update branch)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: vertz-dev-front[bot] <2828126+vertz-dev-front[bot]@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat(lmx): add opta-urgency-v1 classifier for priority routing on ANE

Phase 4 second model: BERT-Tiny urgency classifier with 4 levels
(casual/normal/important/urgent). 100% val accuracy, 0.34ms mean
ANE latency. Runs alongside intent classifier for parallel query
analysis.

- Training pipeline: 4,000 synthetic examples, BERT-Tiny + Linear(128,4)
- CoreML backend: subprocess-isolated ANE inference (urgency_classifier.py)
- Integrated into /v1/chat/completions pipeline
- Added to /admin/ane/status and feedback logger
- Config: urgency_classifier_enabled, model_path, tokenizer_id, max_length
- Validated on Mono512: casual=99%, urgent=99.7% confidence

commit.message:
Add async directory loading with spinner feedback

navigate_to now reads directory entries in a background thread,
showing a loading spinner while waiting. This prevents UI freezes
when switching to slow drives (HDD, network).

outside the label so arrow clicks only fire the moveSeat handler.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: MCP ergonomics — startup header, status count, research close, early-commit resilience (#61)

* fix: show MCP provider info instead of local path in startup header (#56)

When an MCP tasklist provider is configured, the startup banner now
displays the provider name and labels instead of the local file path.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat: show live task counts in --status for MCP providers

Fetch open/completed task counts from remote MCP providers in
analyze_tasklist() instead of returning zeros. Degrades gracefully
when the remote fetch fails. Also add explicit DO NOT COMMIT
instruction to the builder prompt.

Generated with millstone orchestrator

* fix: use MCP task ID and close remote tasks in research mode

When the tasklist is MCP-backed, run_single_task() now derives the
current task title and ID from the remote provider's cached task list
instead of reading a local file that may not exist or be stale. After
research mode completes, the remote task is closed via
update_task_status() so MCP-backed tasks don't remain open.

Generated with millstone orchestrator

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: detect and handle builder early commits

When the builder agent commits its own changes (advancing HEAD), the
orchestrator now detects this and uses the committed diff for review
instead of expecting unstaged changes. Skips redundant commit delegation
and auto-commits the tasklist checkbox if the builder left it unstaged.

Handles edge cases: review-fix cycles with uncommitted changes after an
early commit, LoC baseline updates, and git failures during tasklist
auto-commit.

Generated with millstone orchestrator

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

---------

commit.message:
feat: add Plotter.view_vector() to point the camera in a given direction (#88)

* feat: add Plotter.view_vector matching PyVista API

Add view_vector(vector, viewup=None) to Plotter, VTKJSRenderer, and
MockRenderer. After resetCamera(), the generated JS sets the camera
position to focal_point + vector and applies the viewup, then calls
resetCameraClippingRange() to fix near/far planes.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* test: add tests for view_vector

Cover Plotter.view_vector and renderer view_vector in both
test_plotter.py and test_rendering.py:
- vector and viewup stored on MockRenderer and VTKJSRenderer
- default viewup is (0, 1, 0)
- generated HTML contains setPosition, setViewUp, getFocalPoint,
  and resetCameraClippingRange with correct values
- no camera code in HTML when view_vector is not called
- parametrized test for common viewing directions

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* [pre-commit.ci] auto fixes from pre-commit.com hooks

for more information, see https://pre-commit.ci

commit.message:
Fix oil chart: reject stale AlphaVantage data during extreme price events

AV free BRENT API has 1-3 day lag. After Iran war spike (+26% weekly),
AV returns ~$77 (last week) and overwrites the correct $92.86 in STATIC_DATA.

Guard added: if live value is >15% below static value, skip the AV update.
This prevents stale data from overwriting accurate extreme-event prices.

commit.message:
fix: live subtitles in full-duplex via inline WS recv

Replace the separate _ws_reader thread with inline non-blocking
ws.recv() calls in the recording thread after each audio send.
This follows the same pattern as stt_streaming() and avoids
thread-safety issues with websocket-client concurrent send/recv.
Hypothesis messages update stt_partial[0] which the progress
display reads for live subtitles.

commit.message:
feat: add Codex CLI as direct worker type for per-persona interactive sessions

Add a dedicated codex-worker that manages OpenAI Codex CLI directly (bypassing
pi-agent-core), enabling users to have multiple Codex personas each with their
own persistent thread. Real-time JSONL events stream through IPC → PubNub to
the browser.

Phase 1: Fix Codex event handler to normalize snake_case/camelCase event types,
extend buildCodexArgs with persona instructions support.

Phase 2: Add WorkerType ('pi-agent' | 'codex') throughout the stack — IPC types,
session actor, DB (worker_type + codex_thread_id columns), PendingMessage,
PubNub types, and codex config schema.

Phase 3: Create codex-worker.ts as a new build entry point. SessionActorManager
selects the worker script based on actor.workerType. Orchestrator persists Codex
thread IDs to SQLite for crash recovery. Steering falls through to queue for
Codex workers (no mid-turn injection).

Also replaces milo-install with milobot-install — a more robust installer that
auto-installs Node.js and pnpm if missing.

commit.message:
fix: stale peak check runs after buildLivePortfolio, threshold lowered to 2x

Previously the stale check ran before buildLivePortfolio, so currentTotal
was computed from unpriced holdings (valueUsd=0). This made the check always
fail (currentTotal=0 short-circuits the condition), allowing a stale $21K
peak to persist and halt all trading with a fake 99% drawdown.

Fix: move stale check to after buildLivePortfolio where totalValueUsd is
correctly priced. Also lower the staleness threshold from 10x to 2x — if
the stored peak is more than double the live portfolio, it needs resetting.
Also reset data/peak-value.json to clear the immediate block.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Classify AssignmentPatternAsAggregate → §10.9.2 [skip ci]

The test assigns '{1, 2} to a struct variable of type point_t, which is a positional structure assignment pattern. §10.9.2 (Structure assignment patterns) is the most specific subclause defining this syntax. The comment's cited §11.2.2 does not exist in IEEE 1800-2023.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Filter non-physical places from Google Places API results (#89)

* Filter out online/non-physical businesses from Places results (NES-211)

Google Places API returns listings for online-only services (FreshDirect,
Instacart, etc.) and permanently closed businesses. These pollute
provisioning, third-place, fitness, and other lifestyle scores by
counting as nearby amenities when no physical storefront exists.

Adds centralized filtering in places_nearby() and text_search() that
removes: permanently closed businesses (via business_status field) and
known online-only services (via exact name match and regex patterns).

https://claude.ai/code/session_016ed1HFDgHdDYYCFJetbXuM

* Add trace logging for filtered non-physical places (NES-211)

Individual filtered places logged at DEBUG (name, status, place_id)
for false-positive auditing. Aggregate count logged at INFO so it
appears in normal evaluation traces without noise.

https://claude.ai/code/session_016ed1HFDgHdDYYCFJetbXuM

* Add structured filter reasons and trace integration (NES-211)

_non_physical_reason() now returns a typed reason string:
- "closed_permanently" for Google business_status flag
- "online_exact:<name>" for exact name matches
- "online_pattern:<regex>" for regex pattern matches

Filtered places are attached to the TraceContext as filtered_places
and included in full_trace_dict() output, so they appear in the
builder dashboard for false-positive auditing.

https://claude.ai/code/session_016ed1HFDgHdDYYCFJetbXuM

* Update CLAUDE.md with NES-211 patterns: centralized Places filtering and auditable heuristic filters

https://claude.ai/code/session_016ed1HFDgHdDYYCFJetbXuM

commit.message:
Improve yolo file cleanup + add regression test + testing rule

Yolo fix improvement: setYoloMode() now ALWAYS syncs the .yolo file
to disk, even when the boolean value hasn't changed. Previously the
guard (m_yoloMode != enabled) skipped file cleanup when called with
false on a session already at false — leaving stale files that caused
ghost auto-approvals. Also removed the redundant cleanup in run()
since setYoloMode now handles it.

New test: testSetYoloMode_CleansStaleFileWhenAlreadyOff — creates a
stale .yolo file, calls setYoloMode(false) on a session already at
false, verifies the file is removed.

CLAUDE.md: Added mandatory testing rule — every feature, bug fix, or
behavioral change MUST have an automated test before it's complete.

feat: AI Chatbot 예시 질문 카드 & 입력 UI 개선 (#37)

* [#36] 예시 질문 카드 & 입력 UI 개선

* 블로그별 예시 질문 상수 파일 추가 (blog-v2, investment, inspireme)
* MessageList 빈 화면에 아이콘 + 예시 질문 카드 UI 구현
* 예시 질문 클릭 시 자동 전송 연결
* ChatInput을 Google Scholar 스타일 둥근 입력창 + 내부 Send 아이콘으로 개선

commit.message:
Polish Excel export: Helvetica Neue, DD-MM-YYYY dates, INR currency, thick section borders

- All cells use Helvetica Neue font
- Dates formatted as DD-MM-YYYY
- Amount columns use ₹#,##0.00 number format
- Thick medium borders between sections for visual separation
- Alternating row colors (white/light gray)
- Amounts right-aligned, dates center-aligned

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

repository.name: InvoiceProcessor
repository.description: Personal Portal for Automation

commit.message:
Apply numbered ordering to guide folder structure

폴더와 파일 모두에 번호를 붙여 GitHub/IDE에서도 읽기 순서가 보이도록 변경.
SUMMARY.md는 여전히 GitBook 사이드바 순서를 제어.

- 1-platform/, 2-ai-gateway/, 3-operator/ (최상위)
- 01-getting-started/, 02-members/, ... (하위 폴더)
- 01-concepts.md, 02-roles.md, ... (파일)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

repository.name: ugdp
repository.description: User Guide Driven Planning (UGDP): 사용자 가이드를 기획의 main branch로 두고, 각 pitch를 그 문서에 대한 PR로 정의하는 기획 방식.

commit.message:
feat: Phase 1 — dark mode, save indicator, responsive layout, security

- Add dark mode toggle with system preference detection
- Add auto-save indicator (Saving.../Saved/Error) in header
- Make all components responsive for mobile (stacking grids, touch-friendly actions)
- Add rate limiting on auth endpoints (10 req/15min)
- Add input sanitization (email normalization, payload size limit)
- Add password reset flow (token-based)
- Add project roadmap document

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

L02語彙を10語追加（去・課・下午・上午・下課・上課・要・快樂・起床・飯）

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: auto-fallback between Gemini and Claude on rate limit

When Gemini hits daily quota, automatically switches to Claude and
notifies the user. Retries Gemini every 5 minutes and switches back
when available. Both providers must have API keys set for fallback
to work.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
